title: ARCHIVED – Supplementary Data on Budget 2012 Implementation - Canada.ca
content:
## ARCHIVED – Supplementary Data on Budget 2012 Implementation

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.

## We have archived this page and will not be updating it.
You can use it for research or reference.
Program Alignment Architecture
1.1 Permanent Economic Residents
1.2 Temporary Economic Residents
2.1 Family and Discretionary Immigration
2.2 Refugee Protection
3.1 Settlement and Integration of Newcomers
3.2 Citizenship for Newcomers and all Canadians
3.3 Multiculturalism for Newcomers and all Canadians
4.1 Health Management [ Note 1 ]
4.2 Migration Control and Security Management
4.3 Canadian Influence on International Migration and Integration agenda
5.1 Internal Services
- [1] Interim Federal Health Program ( IFHP ) changes include: ending coverage, other than that needed to protect public health and safety, for rejected asylum claimants, and ending supplemental benefits for most non-government sponsored beneficiaries. This ensures IFHP benefits are not more generous than what Canadians receive through government-funded benefit programs. A description of IFHP benefits is on CIC’s web site . [ back to note 1 ]
